name: Logikcull Cloud E-Discovery API
what_it_does: Logikcull self-service cloud e-discovery REST API for small to mid-size law firms, corporate legal departments, and government agencies to manage document collection, processing, and review without e-discovery vendor overhead. Enables AI agents to manage matter creation and upload processing for self-service e-discovery workflow automation, handle document review and tagging for legal review automation, access production and export management for litigation document production automation, retrieve search and filter capabilities for legal document analysis automation, manage team member and reviewer access for legal collaboration automation, handle legal hold management and custodian notification for litigation readiness automation, access analytics and review progress tracking for litigation cost management automation, retrieve native file and TIFF production options for litigation production format automation, manage saved searches and smart folders for legal document organization automation, and integrate Logikcull with law firm practice management for self-service e-discovery workflow integration.
use_cases: Processing litigation documents from self-service e-discovery automation agents
Managing legal holds from litigation readiness automation agents
Producing documents from small firm litigation production agents
Tracking review progress from legal cost management agents
not_for: Large-scale enterprise e-discovery with millions of documents (use Relativity or DISCO)
AI-assisted predictive coding for large review sets (use Everlaw or DISCO)
Complex structured data collection and analysis
best_when: A small to mid-size law firm, boutique litigation practice, or corporate legal department wanting AI agents to automate self-service e-discovery document processing, legal hold management, and document production without large e-discovery vendor engagement overhead.
avoid_when: VOLUME LIMITS FOR LARGE CASE E-DISCOVERY: Logikcull self-service model has practical scale limits for very large matters (10M+ documents); automated e-discovery workflow for large complex matters should evaluate platform capacity before deployment; automated batch processing for very large matters may encounter processing queue delays and cost thresholds. ATTORNEY OVERSIGHT FOR REVIEW DECISIONS: Automated document review tagging via Logikcull must include attorney oversight for relevance and privilege decisions; automated tagging without attorney review creates review quality risk for production obligations. DEFENSIBLE LEGAL HOLD PROCESS DOCUMENTATION: Automated legal hold notice via Logikcull must maintain defensible documentation of hold issuance, custodian receipt confirmation, and hold compliance; automated hold without audit trail creates spoliation exposure if custodian fails to preserve.

auth.notes: Logikcull uses API key authentication. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2004 by Andy Wilson and Santosh Varughese. Acquired by Reveal (formerly Cicayda) in 2022. Self-service cloud e-discovery for small firms and corporate legal. Simple upload, process, review, produce model. Fixed-rate pricing per matter. SOC2 Type II. Competes with DISCO, Everlaw, and Casepoint for cloud e-discovery.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier for small matters under 1GB; limited production features
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-matter flat-rate pricing based on data volume; subscription plans for frequent users; API access included in paid plans
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. Reveal subsidiary since 2022. Per-matter flat-rate pricing. Free tier under 1GB. Subscription for frequent users.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; document and matter queries throttled; webhooks for processing and review events

known_agent_gotchas: REVEAL ACQUISITION PLATFORM MIGRATION: Logikcull was acquired by Reveal in 2022; Reveal platform consolidation may affect Logikcull API availability and roadmap as Reveal integrates Logikcull capabilities into the Reveal platform; evaluate Logikcull API stability and deprecation timeline before building long-term automated workflows

API KEY FULL ACCOUNT ACCESS WITHOUT SCOPE: Logikcull API key provides access to all matters in the account without scope controls; automated agents with API key can access all client matter documents; implement dedicated per-matter API key if Logikcull supports it; otherwise implement application-level access control for multi-client automated e-discovery workflows

PROCESSING ASYNC COMPLETION POLLING: Logikcull document processing after upload is asynchronous; automated workflow must poll matter processing status or use webhook for processing complete notification; automated review workflow that triggers before processing completion accesses incomplete document set

PRODUCTION QUALITY CONTROL BEFORE AUTOMATED DELIVERY: Automated document production via Logikcull must include production quality control check before delivery to opposing counsel; production with incorrect bates range, missing documents, or format errors creates production deficiency requiring court-supervised correction; implement pre-delivery production validation in automated workflow

FLAT-RATE PRICING MODEL INCENTIVE FOR SCOPE CREEP: Logikcull per-matter flat-rate pricing may create incentive to over-collect documents into a single matter; automated collection without proportionality analysis inflates per-matter cost; design automated collection scope to match FRCP Rule 26 proportionality requirements and separate distinct matters to optimize per-matter pricing

LEGAL HOLD CUSTODIAN ACKNOWLEDGMENT TRACKING: Automated legal hold issuance via Logikcull must track custodian acknowledgment response; automated hold without acknowledgment tracking creates defensibility gap if custodian claims no knowledge of hold obligation; implement acknowledgment reminder and escalation for non-responding custodians
agent_readiness.security_notes: Cloud e-discovery. SOC2. API key. US. Litigation documents and legal matter data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-service e-discovery. SOC2. API key. US. Litigation documents and legal matter data.
